title: notifications-in-app — compliance evidence
description: In-app notifications carry the same content categories as the mail transport but travel over authenticated WebSocket / REST rather than SMTP. Compliance surface is (a) consent tier respected (marketing categories require opt-in), (b) auditability of delivery + read state, (c) tenant isolation on the broadcast channel.

controls.evidence: Marketing-category deliveries respect the same NotificationPreference row as mail/push/sms — no separate opt-in for in-app because the user is already inside the authenticated session. When category.consent_tier=marketing_opt_in, the resolver still checks the preference before writing the delivery row.
Transactional in-app deliveries (billing invoices, security alerts) fire regardless of preference — legal basis is the workspace contract.
Broadcast auth middleware (see middleware.json) verifies the authenticated Sanctum session against the channel name. A user cannot subscribe to another user's private channel.
Every REST endpoint requires notifications.viewOwn + workspace-scope resolution. Delivery rows are indexed on (workspace_id, user_id) to make cross-workspace leaks impossible.
Health probe on broadcast connectivity + WebSocket delivery lag feeds alerting.

notes: The delivery + read state audit trail is co-owned with the base notifications module — there is no separate audit-log surface for the in-app transport.
